hypothesis: On the current best frontier point `lowrank_two_stage_residual, K=32, EF=12`, decreasing only the learning rate from `1.6e-3` to `4e-4` may improve optimization stability enough to lower FVU and strengthen the quality anchor without changing sparsity cost.
summary: Run an exploitation follow-up on the current best recipe by holding architecture, K, expansion factor, optimizer, and `AUXK_ALPHA` fixed and changing only `LR` to an untested lower value.
self_review: This follows the exploitation guidance exactly: no new architecture, no EF move, and one parameter changed on the current best frontier recipe. I chose an untested LR sweep point rather than another aux sweep because aux already looked harmful in the low-K regime, while LR tuning is the cleanest remaining lever on the strongest anchor.
notes_to_memory: Round 15 enters exploitation mode on the current best `lowrank_two_stage_residual` anchor at `K=32, EF=12` and changes only `LR` to `4e-4`.
This tests whether the best-FVU point is slightly optimization-limited and can be improved by a more conservative signum step size without changing sparsity or architecture.
next_hypotheses: If `LR=4e-4` improves the `K=32` anchor, continue the LR sweep on the same fixed recipe with another untested value before touching aux.
If it underperforms clearly, try the opposite LR direction (`3.2e-3`) on the same anchor as the next isolated exploitation round.
If LR tuning shows low sensitivity, move to a small aux sweep on the same `K=32, EF=12` anchor with `AUXK_ALPHA=0.01` while keeping all else fixed.
